content:
- Picked up bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns.md` from `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Deep-verified the existing plan (it ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`) using explorer subagents:
  - confirmed `tests/bdd_api_http.rs` used `read_to_end` + `status_line.contains("...")`
  - confirmed server always writes `Content-Length` and `Connection: close` today (`src/api/worker.rs`), but tests should not depend on EOF framing.
  - changed plan to include strict framed reads + exact numeric status, and extended scope to also fix `src/api/worker.rs` test helpers.
- Implemented strict framed HTTP response reader + parser (no `read_to_end`, no substring status checks):
  - `tests/bdd_api_http.rs`: new `read_http_response_framed`, strict `HTTP/1.1 <3-digit>` status parsing, Content-Length framing, per-request timeout, and updated all BDD tests.
  - added negative parser regression test rejecting 4-digit status codes.
  - `src/api/worker.rs` test module: updated `send_plain_request`/`send_tls_request` helpers to use framed reads and return a typed response with numeric `status_code`, and updated all call sites.
- Ran required gates and stored logs under `.ralph/evidence/bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns/`:
  - `make check` OK
  - `make test` OK (includes `tests/bdd_api_http.rs` with new negative test)
  - `make test-long` OK
  - `make lint` OK (clippy restrictions enforced; no unwrap/expect/panic)
- Marked task header as `<status>done</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>` and ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`.
- Next: commit + push with message `task finished bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns: ...`.

- Completed implementation + verification for bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns.
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed (`3e1ad2d`) and pushed to `master`.
- Working tree should be clean aside from updated progress logs.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and searched for any failing tasks (`<passes>false</passes>` / `<passing>false</passing>`): none found.
- Found a recurring meta-task with `<passes>meta-task</passes>`: `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Per mandatory meta-task rule, selected that meta-task and wrote its path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next: run the meta-task 18 deep skeptical review (and keep `<passes>meta-task</passes>` as instructed).

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Enforced meta-task preflight gate: `.ralph/model.txt` was `normal_high`, updated it to `deep_review`.
- Documented a new preflight-only entry in the meta-task under `## Exploration` (pass-5) and stopped before any substantive review as required.
- Next: restart under `deep_review` and then do the full skeptical review (use explorer subagents in parallel), write a detailed plan with `TO BE VERIFIED`, then proceed per task contract.

- Ran required startup progress read; then scanned repo for forbidden patterns panic!/unwrap/expect/todo!/unimplemented! plus clippy allow/expect suppressions.
- Focused scan on project code paths (src, tests, examples, scripts, Makefile, Cargo.toml) and excluded noisy .ralph history from primary findings.
- Found zero forbidden runtime/test/examples/build-script occurrences.
- Found zero clippy allow/expect suppression attributes in code (no #[allow(clippy::...)] or #[expect(clippy::...)]).
- Verified lint posture still strict: src/lib.rs denies unwrap/expect/panic/todo/unimplemented; Makefile runs clippy with explicit -D for these lints across lib/tests/all-targets.
- Checked for newly introduced matches versus branch base (origin/master merge-base) and working tree diff excluding .ralph; no new occurrences.
- Next step if asked: add an automated guard target that fails on regex hits in tracked code paths before clippy for faster feedback.

- Reviewed src/dcs/{etcd_store,store,worker,state}. Focused on reconnect/resnapshot queue semantics, reset markers, and stale PUT resurrection risks.
- Confirmed current design: reconnect snapshot emits WatchOp::Reset and uses replace_watch_events(clear+extend) to drop queued pre-reconnect events once bootstrap_snapshot succeeds. refresh_from_etcd_watch handles Reset by clearing members/leader/switchover/init_lock.
- Found suspicious gap: when watch/session drops, queued events are not proactively purged immediately; stale pre-disconnect PUTs can still be drained/applied before reconnect snapshot arrives. NotTrusted mitigates trust but cache can temporarily resurrect keys. Worth a defensive drop-on-disconnect marker or epoch gating.
- Found suspicious gap: WatchEvent.revision is carried but never enforced in refresh path; out-of-order/duplicate/stale events can still apply if they enter queue (future regression risk, weak invariant enforcement).
- Coverage review: good real-etcd tests for reconnect empty/non-empty snapshot and queue replacement; missing explicit tests for disconnect-before-resnapshot stale queue drain race and mixed batches where reset is not first.
- Ran targeted unit tests: cargo test -q refresh_reset_clears_cached_records_but_preserves_config (pass) and cargo test -q refresh_put_then_reset_then_put_keeps_only_post_reset_state (pass).
- Next if continuing: add deterministic unit/integration tests for stale-event-after-disconnect race + reset ordering assertions; consider implementation change to clear/drop queue immediately on reconnect trigger and/or add revision/epoch filtering in refresh/apply path.

- Reviewed real-binary enforcement paths in Makefile, src/test_harness/binaries.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs, and src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs.
- Confirmed no #[ignore] tests in src; long tests are selected by Makefile ULTRA_LONG_TESTS with list preflight + --exact execution.
- Found bypass surface: make variable override lets caller replace/empty ULTRA_LONG_TESTS and pass with ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1.
- Found installer trust issue: tools/install-postgres16.sh prefers command -v first when resolving binary links, so PATH poisoning can link wrappers into .tools/postgres16/bin.
- Confirmed runtime real-binary gating is strong once start_cluster executes: require_pg16_process_binaries_for_real_tests + require_etcd_bin_for_real_tests validate existence/regular-file/executable (unix).
- Noted intentional optional behavior in e2e_no_quorum_fencing... integrity probe can log "skipped" when no node reachable, test still passes by design.
- Next useful action: enforce immutable ULTRA_LONG_TESTS in Makefile (override guard) and add explicit preflight target verifying expected .tools symlink realpaths + executable bits before running test-long; then add strace-based execve evidence recipe for CI/local verification.

- Reviewed Makefile test/test-long logic end-to-end, including expansion (`make -n`), ULTRA_LONG_TESTS list, skip args, preflight listing, and per-test exact execution.
- Ran `cargo test --all-targets -- --list` and confirmed all 4 ULTRA_LONG_TESTS names exist exactly as listed; preflight parsing with `awk -F': ' ... | grep -Fx` currently matches correctly.
- Verified current `--skip` tokens each match exactly 1 listed test (no current collateral skips), but noted future silent-omission risk because cargo `--skip` is substring-based.
- Found meaningful silent-confidence risk inside one ultra-long test body: no-quorum fencing integrity check is best-effort and can log "skipped" yet still pass (`assert_table_key_integrity_best_effort` returns None path).
- Noted env gate: `ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` allows make test-long to pass with zero ultra-long tests configured by design; should be constrained in CI policy.
- Next: propose fail-closed verification workflow: enforce non-empty ULTRA list in CI, assert exact list parity with discovered long tests, capture executed-count evidence per test, and make no-quorum integrity probe required (or split required-vs-best-effort tests).

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Verified `.ralph/model.txt` is `deep_review` (precondition satisfied for substantive meta-task pass).
- Spawned 4 explorer subagents to inform a fresh pass-5 plan:
  - Nash: scanned for panic/unwrap/expect/todo/unimplemented + clippy suppression; none found.
  - Pascal/Bacon: reviewed Makefile + real-binary enforcement; flagged `make test-long` override/ALLOW_EMPTY leakage risk and `.tools` install PATH-poisoning risk; also flagged best-effort integrity assertion in HA e2e.
  - Carson: reviewed etcd reconnect/watch semantics; flagged potential window where queued pre-disconnect watch events may still mutate cache while unhealthy before reconnect reset batch lands.
- Appended a detailed `2026-03-04 (fresh run, pass-5) — Plan` to the meta-task and ended it with `TO BE VERIFIED` as required.
- Next: start a new turn, read `TO BE VERIFIED`, perform deep skeptical verification of the plan (must change at least one plan item), then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE` and execute.

- Subagent pass-5 skeptical verification focused on Makefile test targets and override leak risks.
- Inspected Makefile and validated an actual fail-open path: `make test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS= ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` exits success with zero tests.
- Confirmed command-line variable precedence risk against `ULTRA_LONG_TESTS :=` canonical list, and noted `make test` uses substring `--skip` filters that can skip unintended tests if names contain ultra-long identifiers as substrings.
- Proposed concrete plan changes: harden Makefile with `override ULTRA_LONG_TESTS := ...`, remove/relocate ALLOW_EMPTY success path from `test-long`, add adversarial override negative tests in the pass-5 verification plan, and add skip-collision preflight on `cargo test -- --list` before `make test`.
- Next for parent: patch pass-5 plan in task file to include these checks and then continue TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE flow.

- Subagent deep-skeptical verification focused on DCS reconnect semantics in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs, src/dcs/store.rs, src/dcs/worker.rs.
- Confirmed suspected issue is real at cache mutation/publication layer: worker applies drained watch events even when store_healthy=false (worker.rs step_once lines ~76-105), so stale pre-disconnect events can transiently mutate cache before reconnect Reset batch arrives.
- Verified reconnect reset replacement is implemented in etcd_store bootstrap_snapshot via replace_watch_events + WatchOp::Reset (etcd_store.rs ~421-470), which prevents stale replay after reconnect bootstrap, but does not prevent pre-reset window.
- Noted HA decision path is mostly protected by trust gate (ha/decide.rs: if trust != FullQuorum enters FailSafe), so risk is primarily transient stale DCS snapshot exposure while NotTrusted, not immediate normal-path leader-follow actions.
- Existing reconnect tests are permissive and currently mask the window by applying non-reset events before reset (etcd_store.rs lines ~1169-1171 and ~1273-1275). Recommend replacing those permissive lines with fail-closed assertions.
- Suggested plan changes: add explicit worker-side reset-gating (await_reset_epoch), add deterministic unit test for unhealthy stale-event drop, tighten reconnect tests to fail if pre-reset batch mutates cache, and add canceled/compacted watch regression test that asserts no pre-reset mutation.
- Next for parent: patch pass-5 plan TO BE VERIFIED section by altering at least one item: narrow issue statement from broad "resurrect keys" to specific "transient stale cache mutation while NotTrusted" and add concrete checklist/test bullets above.

- Subagent review for meta-task 18 pass-5 focused on HA e2e + BDD realism false-pass risk.
- Confirmed BDD HTTP tests are now strict-framed + numeric status checks (`read_http_response_framed`, `assert_eq!(status_code, ...)`), no obvious optional-pass branches remain in `tests/bdd_api_http.rs` and `tests/bdd_state_watch.rs`.
- Found actionable HA e2e false-pass risks in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` and `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`:
  - no-quorum fencing test can pass while key-integrity probe is skipped when no PG node reachable;
  - strict-all-nodes no-quorum test does a final best-effort poll and ignores per-node errors;
  - partition chaos split-brain windows still pass closed-open (no successful sample requirement).
- Proposed plan changes: add strict sampling/evidence gating tasks, remove/rename best-effort claims for strict tests, and create 2 bug tasks (multi-node strictness + partition-window fail-closed).
- Next for parent: update pass-5 plan TO BE VERIFIED section by altering at least one item, ideally add dedicated remediation item for partition helper fail-closed semantics and enforce non-skippable integrity evidence in no-quorum fencing scenario.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Detected pass-5 plan ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`; performed deep skeptical verification using 4 explorer subagents (Makefile/test-long bypass, real-binary provenance, DCS reconnect/reset window, HA e2e false-pass risks).
- Updated the pass-5 plan with concrete changes:
  - Added explicit `strace` availability precondition.
  - Replaced full `make test-long` tracing with focused single-test execve tracing + required stub-based negative control with `trap` restore.
  - Marked `make test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS= ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` as a CONFIRMED bypass and added required Makefile remediation + adversarial regression check.
  - Narrowed DCS risk wording and added a worker-side reset gate + deterministic test requirement.
  - Added HA e2e confirmed best-effort integrity skip finding + partition-chaos zero-sample fail-open risk.
- Replaced plan marker `TO BE VERIFIED` -> `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: start a new run; since the plan now says `NOW EXECUTE`, execute it without further exploration and tick boxes as you go; then run all gates (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`) to 100% green.

- Traced tool/install scripts and .tools acquisition flow for mdBook/mermaid, etcd, and postgres16 in Makefile/scripts.
- Focused on PATH injection and deterministic acquisition risks (downloaded payload integrity, resolver trust, version pinning, artifact provenance).
- Highest risks found in postgres installer’s `command -v`-based source resolution and etcd downloader lacking integrity verification.
- No code edits made in this turn; prepared a risk-ranked, non-invasive hardening checklist for immediate remediation.
- Next: if requested, implement minimal fixes with version/path allow-list checks, checksum/signature verification for etcd, and strict install preflight validators for docs and real-binary paths.

- Performed focused security/quality review of Makefile test and test-long targets for silent-pass and override bypass risks.
- Verified exact Makefile logic at lines 6-12 and 39-63 and reproduced bypass behavior with commands, including:
  - `make test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='' ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` exits 0 without running tests.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='ha::e2e_multi_node'` generates broad `--skip ha::e2e_multi_node` filter.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=''` drops all `--skip` filters entirely.
  - `make -n test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='<single-test>'` shows runner can be reduced to a partial subset.
- Quantified skip blast radius from cargo --list: current four skip tokens each match exactly 1 test today, but broad token examples would match many tests (e.g., `ha::e2e_multi_node` matched 15).
- Assessed that preflight `cargo test -- --list` + `grep -Fx` in test-long is good for exact existence but does not enforce immutable canonical list, so caller overrides can still redefine scope.
- Next if continuing: implement fail-closed policy in Makefile (block ULTRA_LONG_TESTS/ALLOW_EMPTY overrides in CI or entirely), replace substring skip behavior with exact-set computation, and add a preflight guard target that asserts skip tokens map 1:1 and ultra list equals canonical expected set.

- Reviewed HA e2e and BDD surfaces specifically for false-pass behavior around best-effort integrity checks, transport fallback paths, and timeout/poll loops.
- Confirmed high-severity false-pass in no-quorum fencing stress test: integrity verification is best-effort and explicitly logs/skips when no node reachable, yet scenario still returns passed.
- Found additional weak-evidence pass paths: partition split-brain window assertion can pass with zero successful API samples; partition stable-primary election can accept a primary based on partial node visibility while other nodes are in error.
- Found matrix no-quorum assertion path that is intentionally best-effort (any FailSafe + no observed primary) and can pass with partial visibility/errors, so it does not prove cluster-wide invariant.
- Observed fallback helper (`get_ha_state_with_fallback`) has no direct tests in util module; fallback branches are used by e2e polling and can mask transport failures without explicit evidence that all nodes were observed.
- Next step after context reset: propose concrete code changes to fail closed (required visibility thresholds, strict variant usage), then add focused unit/integration tests that force transport errors/timeouts and verify assertions fail when evidence is insufficient.

- Reviewed DCS etcd watch/reconnect flow in src/dcs/{etcd_store,store,worker,state}. Focused on disconnect window stale-event application, reset marker semantics, revision ordering, and publish behavior while NotTrusted.
- Confirmed reconnect uses bootstrap snapshot + synthetic WatchOp::Reset and replace_watch_events(clear+extend), but queue is only replaced after bootstrap succeeds; stale queued events can still be drained/applied before reconnect reset arrives.
- Confirmed WatchEvent.revision is populated but never enforced in refresh/apply path; no last-applied revision or epoch watermark exists, so out-of-order stale events are not rejected.
- Confirmed DCS worker publishes full cache even when store unhealthy and trust=NotTrusted; HA currently gates on trust first, but safety depends on all consumers honoring trust.
- Coverage exists for reset behavior and queue replacement on reconnect, but tests do not explicitly forbid transient stale application during disconnect/reconnect window and do not assert revision monotonic filtering invariants.
- Next if continuing: implement fail-closed ordering invariants (epoch+revision floor), clear/drop stale queue on disconnect trigger, and add deterministic tests for stale pre-reconnect drain race + out-of-order revision rejection + NotTrusted cache-consumer gating.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Loaded the meta-task file; it previously ended with `NOW EXECUTE` and contained a pass-4 oriented plan.
- Ran parallel skeptical subagents to refresh the risk surface:
  - Makefile silent-pass/override bypasses (`ULTRA_LONG_TESTS`, `ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS`, substring `--skip`).
  - DCS etcd disconnect-window stale-event application + missing revision monotonicity + permissive Reset protocol.
  - HA e2e false-pass branches (best-effort integrity, zero-sample split-brain checks, partial-observability stable-primary).
  - Tool install trust holes (PATH-poisonable postgres resolver, etcd installer missing checksum verification).
- Updated the task markdown with a new detailed **pass-5 plan** incorporating the above and replaced the end marker with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Did NOT execute any fixes or run gates yet (per workflow: plan -> TO BE VERIFIED -> stop).

- Next: deep skeptical verification of the plan (must change at least one plan item), then switch marker to `NOW EXECUTE` and only then execute + run `make check/test/test-long/lint`.

- Inspected Makefile test and test-long targets plus ULTRA_LONG_TESTS/ALLOW_EMPTY logic.
- Reproduced bypasses with make -n and live run:
  - `make test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='' ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` exits 0 without running tests.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=''` removes all --skip filters.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='ha::e2e_multi_node'` creates broad substring skip token.
  - `make -n test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='<single test>'` narrows executed long suite to one test.
- Ran `cargo test --all-targets -- --list` snapshot and measured substring blast radius:
  - `ha::e2e_multi_node` currently matches 15 tests; `e2e_multi_node` also 15; `no_quorum` 2.
  - Canonical four ULTRA_LONG_TESTS names each currently match exactly 1 test.
- Next: provide user with exact proof commands, fail-closed Makefile patch plan, and deterministic preflight validation recipe using one list snapshot + exact-name checks + exact execution.

- Inspected tools/install-postgres16.sh and tools/install-etcd.sh in detail with line numbers, plus compared to tools/install-mdbook.sh as a stronger integrity baseline.
- Confirmed no additional helper script is referenced directly by the two target installers; provenance/integrity behavior is self-contained in those scripts.
- Found concrete gaps:
  - install-postgres16.sh resolves binaries via command -v before fixed system paths, allowing PATH-poisoned wrappers to be symlinked into .tools/postgres16/bin.
  - install-postgres16.sh does not verify linked binaries are package-owned trusted paths nor enforce major version 16 across all tools.
  - install-etcd.sh downloads and extracts release archives with no checksum/signature verification and weak version input validation.
  - install-etcd.sh uses persistent download dir extraction without robust stale-dir cleanup/atomic install checks.
- Prepared a concrete hardening plan: strict PATH policy + absolute command paths, deterministic binary resolution, package ownership/version validation for postgres, pinned-version/checksum (or GPG+checksums) for etcd, safer extraction/atomic move, and explicit post-install self-checks.
- Prepared test plan including negative controls: PATH poisoning harness, tampered archive checksum fail, wrong-version reject, malformed version arg reject, missing expected binary fail, and stale extraction residue checks.
- Next: if asked to implement, patch both scripts and add an automated installer self-test target that runs these negative controls in CI/local shells.

- Inspected HA e2e fail-open/optional assertion surfaces in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`, and polling helper `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs`.
- Confirmed concrete weak-evidence paths:
  - partition helper `wait_for_stable_primary` can elect a primary from partial visibility because it consumes `cluster_ha_states_best_effort` and accepts `primaries.len()==1` even when other nodes return errors.
  - partition `assert_no_dual_primary_window` can pass with zero successful samples because it never tracks success count and accepts timeout as pass.
  - multi-node no-quorum matrix path still uses `wait_for_no_primary_and_any_failsafe_best_effort` (any failsafe + no observed primary) and can pass under partial observability.
  - fencing scenario still allows key-integrity verification to be skipped (`assert_table_key_integrity_best_effort` returns `None`) and then marks scenario passed.
- Checked polling fallback helper `get_ha_state_with_fallback`: behavior is fail-closed when both paths fail, but there is no dedicated test coverage for transport-timeout fallback branches and no evidence accounting that callers observed all nodes.
- Next step prepared: provide a severity-ordered review with exact file:line refs and concrete plan edits + regression tests focused on fail-closed observability requirements.

- Inspected DCS etcd reconnect/watch semantics in src/dcs/{etcd_store.rs,worker.rs,store.rs} with line-level trace.
- Confirmed disconnect window: run_worker_loop marks unhealthy + drops watch handles on stream errors (etcd_store.rs ~274-296) but does not clear pending events queue at disconnect; queue replacement only happens later in bootstrap_snapshot on successful reconnect (etcd_store.rs ~465-467).
- Confirmed worker always drains/applies events regardless current store health (worker.rs ~89-105), so pre-break queued PUTs can mutate cache during reconnect gap before Reset batch lands.
- Confirmed revision exists on WatchEvent but is unused in refresh_from_etcd_watch (store.rs ~116-171), so no stale/out-of-order filtering invariant currently.
- Existing reconnect tests assert Reset/replace behavior after reconnect but not the disconnect-gap stale-apply window (etcd_store.rs ~1092+).
- Proposed minimal fix plan: clear pending queue immediately whenever watch session is invalidated, keep reconnect bootstrap replace semantics, and add deterministic test using EstablishDelayGuard + forced outage to assert stale queued event is dropped during reconnect gap.
- Optional hardening plan: add revision floor in worker context and ignore events with revision <= last_applied; add unit test for out-of-order events. Keep optional if minimizing surface.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Found pass-5 plan ended with `TO BE VERIFIED`; performed deep skeptical verification using parallel subagents (Makefile gate realism, tool installer integrity, DCS reconnect window, HA e2e false-pass surfaces, plus a fast plan-structure scan).
- Updated the pass-5 plan substantially:
  - Removed duplicate/older pass-5 plan block to avoid execution drift.
  - Added explicit prerequisites (`strace`, `sha256sum`, `file`) and binary identity capture (realpaths, file type, hashes).
  - Added real-binary provenance proof via `strace` including `execveat`, plus a required negative control stub for `.tools/etcd/bin/etcd` with `trap` restore.
  - Strengthened Makefile hardening plan with exact PoCs, skip-substring collision preflight, and explicit override-forbidden checks.
  - Refined DCS plan to include a minimal must-do fix (clear queued events on disconnect) plus a deterministic regression test; left revision-floor/epoch gating as optional hardening after minimal fix is green.
  - Tightened HA e2e remediation plan to fail closed on partial observability / zero samples / skipped integrity proofs, and listed concrete regression tests.
- Replaced the plan marker `TO BE VERIFIED` -> `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next: start a new turn; since the plan now says `NOW EXECUTE`, execute it as written (no more exploration) and tick checkboxes while doing so; then run all gates (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`) to 100% green and follow closeout/commit workflow.

- Audited tool installers for trust/integrity: tools/install-postgres16.sh and tools/install-etcd.sh; compared against install-mdbook.sh and install-mdbook-mermaid.sh patterns.
- Confirmed install-postgres16.sh has PATH poisoning exposure in resolve_bin(): command -v is consulted before trusted absolute paths, then symlinked into .tools.
- Confirmed install-etcd.sh performs unauthenticated archive download/extract/copy with no checksum/signature verification and weak version input constraints.
- Verified no extra helper script is sourced by target installers (only /etc/os-release in postgres installer).
- Next: deliver concrete hardening edits aligned to repo conventions (pinned version/checksum, temp extract+atomic move, strict binary path policy, explicit version/ownership checks).

- Inspected Track C targets: src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs with line-level review for false-pass vectors.
- Confirmed concrete fail-open surfaces:
  - e2e_multi_node.rs: assert_table_key_integrity_best_effort returns Ok(None) when all nodes unreachable; caller in no-quorum fencing scenario treats None as pass (lines ~943-994 and ~2730-2749).
  - e2e_multi_node.rs: scenario matrix still calls wait_for_no_primary_and_any_failsafe_best_effort (line ~2198), allowing pass with partial observability.
  - e2e_partition_chaos.rs: wait_for_stable_primary uses cluster_ha_states_best_effort and can elect stable primary from subset visibility (lines ~193-271).
  - e2e_partition_chaos.rs: assert_no_dual_primary_window can timeout-success with zero successful samples because it has no sample counter / fail-closed finalization (lines ~281-303).
- Confirmed stricter helper exists in multi-node (wait_for_all_nodes_failsafe) but matrix test still uses weaker one.
- Drafted fail-closed change set + tests:
  - Introduce strict integrity assertion that requires at least one successful node probe and make no-quorum fencing scenario require it.
  - Replace matrix no-quorum call with strict all-nodes fail-safe helper.
  - Add strict/full-observability polling helper in partition chaos and use it for stable-primary + dual-primary window checks.
  - Add zero-sample fail-closed unit tests for partition helper logic and a best-effort integrity-none regression test in multi-node.
- Next step if asked to implement: patch helpers + callers, add targeted unit tests under existing #[cfg(test)] sections, then run focused cargo test for both e2e files and broader make test/test-long gates.

- Audited Makefile `test` and `test-long` for fail-open/silent-pass behavior and override realism.
- Reproduced concrete PoCs:
  - `make test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='' ALLOW_EMPTY_ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=1` exits 0 with no tests.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS=''` removes all `--skip` filters.
  - `make -n test ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='ha::e2e_multi_node'` creates broad substring skip.
  - `make -n test-long ULTRA_LONG_TESTS='<single canonical long test>'` narrows long-suite execution to one test.
- Quantified substring blast radius from `cargo test --all-targets -- --list`:
  - `ha::e2e_multi_node` and `e2e_multi_node` currently match 15 tests; `no_quorum` matches 2.
  - Each canonical full ultra-long name matches exactly 1 test currently.
